text:
Once upon a time, in a distant galaxy far, far away, there lived a wise old owl named Orville. Orville's main hobby was reading ancient tomes left behind by forgotten civilizations. One particularly enchanting tome told the story of a forest sanctuary where birds could live forever.

Orville decided that he wanted to be one of those birds. He flew to the library, filled his beak with berries, and read every story about eternal life. One particular narrative spoke of a wise old tree that promised eternal life. Orville fell onto its broad, gnarled branch.

The tree embraced him warmly. "Welcome, feathered friend. I shall be your guardian forever."

Over the years, countless birds passed through Orville's branches. They whispered songs, built nests, and had families. Orville watched them grow old, sick, and pass away peacefully.

One stormy night, a flock of birds desperate for refuge flew close to the tree. Orville hooted softly
